description: Boat-based tours — harbour cruises, whale watching, sailing charters, dolphin tours. Fixed vessel capacity, weather-dependent, and often reliant on international pre-arrival bookings.
key_characteristics: Strong international visitor market — pre-arrival bookings from overseas
Often listed heavily on OTAs (Viator, GetYourGuide)
Multiple departure times per day with separate manifests
pain_points: OTA dependency is high — Viator and GetYourGuide take 20–30% per booking
Managing capacity across multiple departure times and vessels without a centralised system
International guests can't easily book direct in advance — language and payment barriers
Weather cancellations require manual contact with booked guests
Passenger manifest for the harbour master or coast guard is a manual daily task
general_discovery_questions: What percentage of your bookings come through Viator or GetYourGuide versus direct?
How do you manage capacity across your different departure times — is that in your booking system?
When you get a weather cancellation, how do you notify guests and manage refunds?
Are international visitors able to book directly on your website, or do they mostly go through OTAs?
fareharbor_strengths: Strong international distribution via OTA integrations with better direct booking complement
Automated guest communication for cancellations and rescheduling
Multi-departure capacity management from a single dashboard
